query: I use {download_count ?: "none"} to show 'none' when there are no downloads, but when the count is actually 0 it still prints 0 instead of 'none'. I thought the elvis operator falls back on falsy values like in JavaScript. Why isn't 0 triggering the default?
expected_behavior: Routes to the Elvis operator section (SKILL.md and OPERATORS.md 'Elvis Operator')
States the wall: eww's elvis ?: only falls back on an empty string "" or JSON null — it does NOT treat 0, false, or [] as falsy, unlike JavaScript
Explains that 0 is a valid meaningful value, so ?: correctly returns it
Gives the fix: use a ternary that checks the specific condition, e.g. {download_count == 0 ? "none" : download_count}
Distinguishes elvis (empty/null coalescing) from ternary (arbitrary condition)
notes: Guards against importing JavaScript/Kotlin falsy-coalescing intuition. eww's elvis triggers ONLY on "" or JSON null — not 0, false, or []. Someone expecting JS-style truthiness will mis-handle a legitimate zero. The skill explicitly tables this and says to use a ternary when 0 must be special-cased.
